refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide a method to derive, record, and manage performance requirements (e.g., cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) from worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The requirement is already well-defined but needs to be treated as a high-level functional requirement that bridges site-level mission planning with machine-level performance targets.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Demonstrate the generation of a performance baseline report from a simulated worksite mission profile and verify the audit trail of updates.
sub_frs: FR-1: Derive performance targets from mission context.
FR-2: Record and version-control performance baselines.
FR-3: Manage calibration updates based on real-time telemetry.

coupling_check.notes: The IPME acts as a centralized logic layer, minimizing coupling by decoupling the derivation logic from the physical vehicle control.

assumptions_risks: Assumes worksite mission context is available in a machine-readable format.
Risk: High safety criticality requires strict validation of derived targets to ensure they don't exceed physical safety limits.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Ensure the DP includes a validation loop against the Safety Services module.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The top-level system orchestrator that manages the relationship between site-level requirements and sub-system execution.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives machine performance data to update baselines.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Communicates derived performance targets to operational modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Vehicle should execute, not derive high-level mission requirements.
Teleoperation is a consumer of targets, not the manager.
Safety services must constrain the targets but not necessarily derive the productivity metrics.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing this in the NGHE Solution module prevents logic duplication across different vehicle types.
containment_risks: Logic diffusion if vehicle-specific constraints are not properly abstracted.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear API for 'Vehicle' to provide its physical limits to the 'NGHE Solution' module.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The IPME directly addresses the need to derive, record, and manage requirements.

diffusion_risks: Risk of performance logic leaking into the Telemetry interface handling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate container for cross-system performance management.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High; centralized data makes auditing and verification straightforward.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation to the system-level orchestrator.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define the 'SafetyAlert' interface interaction to ensure performance targets never override safety protocols.

decision.justification: The high-level FR and DP are sound, but the complexity of 'deriving' vs 'managing' requires a lower level of decomposition to define the sub-functions.

revise.why: Need to specify the internal components of the IPME in the next level.

assumptions: Site Manager provides mission context via the Tasking interface.
open_risks: Latency in Telemetry might affect the 'real-time' aspect of calibration updates.
evidence_needed: Interface Control Document (ICD) for Tasking and Telemetry.
